body.u: (An old timer looking out of the window) (myself) 1978, leave school, got a summer job between college as I was on the waiting list I remember spent 3 months at the Catering College at Gravesend and was one of the best {four?} of the class so I had the option of going an oil tanker BP was the company, but did not like that for long . So I change to Shell, where they put me right I stayed with Shell for 7 years and then moved to cargo by I was 24 years old I and during that I had worked myself up from bay steward, to captain’s steward to {?} steward, I was earning £1.50 (due to inflation and promotion) and I reckoned another year and I would be chief steward or even further, but my company was laying off 30,000 men so I decided to change cargo which set back in the {of} promotion, but it was a matter of keeping a job during this I had studying city of Guilds Examination so when I left the shipping bussenessbusiness altogether, I would something to fall back on. When I had first started I had sent home £10 each week, and now I sending £30 each week which was going into a bank (as I did not have to for food or accommodation except on leave) and the sum had up to £7740 in 9 years so I had put a deposit on a house on (then £700) and a I had a second hand car, and I lived in a house in Kent which a friend while I was away and paid the basic rates, the gas electricity while I paid the mortgage for he lived while I was away (rest of the money remained the bank) I then changed to oil tanker.
